source_files: runtime code, loaders, tests, and kernels reviewed read-only

corrections_applied.statement: The DB catalog may advance in parallel with H0 when explicitly authorized and read-only; only retention, deletion, and cleanup remain blocked by open G0.
Bastian decides semantic route validity and route policy; Codex resolves the technical snapshot lineage that produced each materialization.
payload_json is REQUIRED_CURRENT_SURFACE and may be replaced only after equivalent parity and consumer migration, not treated as canonical forever.
Specific dates, row counts, and batch numbers are evidence snapshots, not durable horizon rules.
STOCK_ZERO becomes LEGACY_STABLE gradually; it is not obsolete and remains the working app while capabilities are validated.

details.decision_owner: Bastian for semantic policy; Codex for technical lineage

details.cause_class: weekly recomputation from raw/event scope instead of staged daily surface

direction_change_conditions: A horizon may change only after a new validated finding is promoted into AI_SHARED_MEMORY or AI_BACKLOG with evidence and baseline commit.
Any proposal to delete, retain, or archive Supabase raw data requires G0 closed, an authoritative DB catalog, and explicit Bastian approval.
Any proposal to create a new repository, run a big-bang rewrite, or obsolete STOCK_ZERO requires a separate Bastian decision phase.
Any payload_json replacement requires proven equivalent consumers and parity; removal by assumption is rejected.
Any route-snapshot rule must separate semantic policy chosen by Bastian from technical lineage reconstructed by Codex.

bastian_decision_points.question: Which route snapshot policy is semantically authoritative for weeks where materialization and latest route diverge?
When is the local PostgreSQL laboratory safely destroyable and rebuildable from git plus evidence manifests?
Approve scope and timing for separating Inventory and Control Gestion at capability-interface level inside the same repo.
Whether generic temporal-contract capabilities should become product surfaces after evidence-backed decision pack.

horizons.name: Parity Closure and Temporal Contract Foundation
horizons.objective: Close Control Gestion parity and capture the temporal declarations required for deterministic reconstruction.
allowed_work: Read-only validation and local-lab parity diagnosis when explicitly authorized.
Define affected date window, source batch set, route snapshot, precedence version, build version, and staged daily contract as parameters.
Investigate DB catalog and consumers in parallel when the phase is explicitly read-only and no retention or cleanup is attempted.
Document route snapshot lineage and unresolved parity gaps.
forbidden_work: Supabase cleanup, raw retention, raw deletion, archive execution, or payload_json removal.
Runtime refactor, capability extraction, new repository, or big-bang rewrite.
Treating latest raw or latest route as an implicit reconstruction contract.
gates: Daily parity exact for the declared window under expected_parity.
Weekly parity reproduced from staged daily and route surfaces.
Route policy decision recorded separately from technical batch lineage.
All temporal declarations recorded as versioned reconstruction parameters.

horizons.name: Contract Registry and DB Catalog
horizons.objective: Make temporal contracts and DB object dependencies explicit without authorizing retention or deletion.
allowed_work: Version contract declarations as data/config under git.
Build authoritative DB object and consumer catalog under explicit read-only authorization.
Classify raw as necessary, reconstructable, or historical only after parity evidence exists.
forbidden_work: Deleting, archiving, compacting, or retaining raw data by action before G0 is closed and Bastian approves.
Changing loaders or SQL without a separate implementation phase.
gates: Contract registry names all stable parameters and evidence snapshots.
DB catalog includes payload_json, row_hash, route history, and mart consumers at sufficient granularity.
Retention remains design-only until G0 and catalog gates close.

horizons.name: Domain Seam: Inventory and Control Gestion
horizons.objective: Describe Inventory and Control Gestion as independent capabilities behind stable interfaces inside STOCK_ZERO.
allowed_work: Design read-only capability interfaces after contracts and catalog are reliable.
Separate shared Route Master from Control Gestion-only semantics.
Confirm static-only functions with runtime smoke before any cleanup proposal.
forbidden_work: Moving app/db.py responsibilities without explicit implementation approval.
Duplicating shared route data into separate ungoverned domains.
Removing legacy Streamlit consumers before replacement parity exists.
gates: Inventory and Control Gestion interfaces are independently describable and testable.
Shared Route Master ownership and route policy are documented.
Runtime smoke confirms candidate dead code before any cleanup.

horizons.name: Adapter and Persistence Boundaries
horizons.objective: Formalize source adapters and persistence adapters after domain interfaces and temporal contracts are stable.
allowed_work: Define source adapter contracts for Control Gestion sources.
Define persistence adapter contracts for mart staging and materialization.
Keep current loaders as legacy execution paths until explicitly replaced.
forbidden_work: Rewriting loaders or SQL from the map alone.
Treating KPIONE2-specific fields as generic product contract without configuration.
Removing payload_json before equivalent surface and parity are proven.
gates: A new source can be described as adapter input without changing the domain reconstruction contract.
Persistence contract declares build_version and expected parity.
Legacy loader remains guarded unless a specific phase authorizes change.

horizons.name: Headless Capability and Observability Layer
horizons.objective: Expose validated read capabilities without mandatory Streamlit dependency and make runtime fallback visible.
allowed_work: Design a headless read interface for one validated capability.
Design runtime observability for V2/B3 mode and fallback paths.
Keep Streamlit as a stable consumer while headless interfaces are proven.
forbidden_work: Replacing the UI or changing user workflows from this contract alone.
Claiming product readiness before runtime and parity evidence exist.
gates: At least one capability can be described without importing Streamlit.
B3 fallback activation is observable in design and tests.

horizons.name: Product Decision Tower and Legacy Stable Transition
horizons.objective: Prepare an evidence-backed Bastian decision pack for generic reusable capabilities while STOCK_ZERO remains legacy stable.
allowed_work: Define future KPI, alert, and decision/action tracking capabilities as proposals only.
Separate generic temporal-contract engine from Retail Trust-specific configuration and UI.
Plan gradual legacy-stable transition with rollback points.
forbidden_work: Opening a second repository without a separate decision phase.
Implementing product capabilities from a research map alone.
gates: Bastian receives decision pack with options, risks, and evidence.
Any product experiment has explicit scope, approval, and rollback.
No capability is APPROVED or IMPLEMENTED by this horizon file.
